mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which supports corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of prevailing regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 158627k as of t-1mo reflecting continued labor market expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive PAYEMS prints are not followed by above-median forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above a restrictive threshold of 4.0% and stays elevated, tightening financial conditions raise the discount rate on equity cash flows and increase recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 4.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.64% as of t-1mo, approaching restrictive territory amid hiking cycle expectations
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS crossing 4.0% are positive in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional base rate returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of a low and falling unemployment rate combined with a still-low federal funds rate indicates the Fed is behind the tightening curve relative to labor market tightness, raising the probability of accelerated future rate hikes that compress equity valuations over the next 20 trading days, an effect not present when examining either series alone.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.5% while FEDFUNDS below 1.0% relative to a 5-year historical FEDFUNDS median
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% and FEDFUNDS at 3.64% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: labor market overheating amid gradual policy normalization
failure_condition: The interaction term (UNRATE level x FEDFUNDS gap) shows no incremental predictive power over either series individually, measured by a Rank IC improvement below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and foreshadows a broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as markets price in decelerating growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 8% from its trailing 6-month peak
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1246k units as of t-4mo
failure_condition: HOUST declines of this magnitude are followed by positive median 60-day SPX forward returns in more than 55% of historical late-cycle instances, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding real economic output and corporate earnings capacity, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no meaningful short-horizon predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A material deceleration in retail sales growth below a minimal threshold signals weakening consumer demand, which historically precedes downward revisions to corporate earnings guidance and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below 0.1% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 734967 million as of t-2mo
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following triggered RSAFS deceleration episodes are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the signal fails to achieve Rank IC above 0.03 across a 3-year rolling walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a rise in the unemployment rate of at least 0.3 percentage points from its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like signal) indicates the onset of labor market deterioration and recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this relationship is muted or absent in early/mid-cycle regimes where such upticks reflect labor supply growth rather than demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3pp or more from its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% as of t-1mo, near cycle lows
failure_condition: Triggered UNRATE upticks are followed by positive median 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of late-cycle historical instances, or the directional accuracy of the signal is not statistically different from chance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of accelerating CPI inflation alongside decelerating or contracting industrial production constitutes a stagflationary signal that raises uncertainty about both corporate margins and policy response, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premia rise abruptly; this effect requires the co-movement of both series and is not captured by either alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change accelerates above 2.5% while INDPRO MoM growth turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 326.588 as of t-1mo, INDPRO at 102.325 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Instances where both trigger conditions co-occur show no significant negative bias in 5-day forward SPX returns relative to unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction term adds no Rank IC improvement over single-series models.
